Kotlin component1/component2 methods intentionally omitted. getFirst/getSecond renamed to first/second (Scala property style). Functionally complete.
Complete 1:1 port. public var value matches original.

Intentionally skipped — unreferenced by ported code
Empty marker trait. Perfect match to AstAction.java interface.
Trait with firstChild/next methods. Getter prefix removed per convention. Nullable correctly mapped.
Abstract class with nodeType/adapter vals, equals/hashCode. Getters removed per convention. equals uses pattern match instead of strict getClass check (minor, acceptable for abstract class).

All methods ported. Private getAction(H) renamed to getActionFromHandler to avoid overload conflict. nodeClasses returns immutable Set copy vs Java mutable view. processNodeOnly uses var instead of Object[] array trick. Nullable wrapping correct.
1:1 match. Java interface -> Scala trait. Single method setIn preserved. No issues.
1:1 match. Java interface -> Scala trait. Single method set preserved with type param. No issues.
1:1 match. Java interface extending Function -> Scala trait with apply method. Returns Nullable[T] matching Java @Nullable. No issues.

Semantic mismatch: Java returns @NotNull T but Scala returns Nullable[T]. The factory is supposed to guarantee non-null but the Scala version allows null through Nullable. Functionally works because callers cast anyway, but the type contract is weaker than original.

Signature differs: Java overrides apply returning @NotNull T with @Nullable DataHolder param. Scala version returns plain T (correct non-null) but takes Nullable[DataHolder]. Does not override the parent apply(DataHolder) - has a different signature with Nullable[DataHolder]. Method overload rather than override.

Same issue as DataNotNullValueNullableFactory: Scala has apply(Nullable[DataHolder]) which is a different method than parent apply(DataHolder), not an override. Java version overrides the same apply(@Nullable DataHolder) method. Functionally equivalent due to Nullable opaque type erasure.
1:1 match. Java interface extending Supplier[T] -> Scala trait with get method. Does not extend Supplier but that is fine for SSG usage. No issues.

All 4 methods match: aggregate, aggregateActions, clean, invokeAfterSet. Commented-out invokeBeforeSet preserved. invokeAfterSet returns Nullable[Set[Class[?]]] matching Java @Nullable Set<Class<?>>. No issues.

All 8 methods present (getAll, getKeys, contains, get, setIn, getOrCompute, toMutable, toImmutable, toDataSet). Java get() returns @Nullable T, Scala returns T (calls key.get with Nullable(this)). toDataSet logic slightly differs: Java uses simple instanceof checks, Scala adds explicit !isInstanceOf[MutableDataHolder] guard. Companion NULL field present. Minor: deprecated get wraps this in Nullable unnecessarily vs Java which passes this directly.

All 8 methods match: get (deprecated override), getOrCompute, set(DataKey), set(NullableDataKey), remove, setFrom, setAll, setIn, clear. Signatures correctly adapted to Scala with Nullable where needed. No missing methods.

Java has 3 constructors, 8 methods (getName, getFactory, getDefaultValue x2, get, getFrom, toString, equals, hashCode). Scala has 3 constructors matching, plus 5 methods (getDefaultValue(holder), get, getFrom, toString, equals, hashCode). Missing: getName() and getFactory() accessors - replaced by public val fields (name, factory) which is the SSG convention. getDefaultValue() no-arg version missing as standalone method but accessible via val defaultValue. get() logic equivalent. Minor: toString says NullableDataKey for all subclasses (same as Java original).

Java has 4 constructors + 5 override methods (getFactory, getDefaultValue x2, get, set, toString). Scala has 4 constructors + 3 methods (get, set, toString). Missing overrides: getFactory (returns DataNotNullValueFactory cast), getDefaultValue no-arg, getDefaultValue(holder). These are accessible through inherited vals so functionally equivalent. Constructor for (name, defaultKey) uses defaultKey.defaultValue (matching Java getDefaultValue()). All constructor logic matches.

Java has 6 constructors + 4 methods (getDefaultValue x2, get, set, toString). Scala has 5 constructors + 3 methods (get, set, toString). All 6 Java constructors covered: (name,dv,factory), (name,factory-nullable), (name,supplier), (name,defaultKey), (name,defaultValue) mapped to (name) with null default. Missing explicit (name, defaultValue) constructor but the (name) constructor covers null case and (name, dv, factory) primary covers non-null. getDefaultValue overrides not needed due to public vals. toString correctly handles null case.

Java: 12 methods + 2 static helpers. Scala: all present. Instance methods (getAll, getKeys, contains, getOrCompute, aggregate, toMutable, toImmutable, toDataSet, toString, equals, hashCode) all match. Companion object has static methods (aggregateActions, aggregate, merge, registerDataKeyAggregator, isAggregatorRegistered, invokeSetContains) all matching Java. registerDataKeyAggregator uses boundary/break correctly replacing Java return. equals uses pattern match correctly. No stubs or TODOs.

Java: 11 methods + 1 static. Scala: all present. set(DataKey), set(NullableDataKey), setByBase (private helper), setFrom, setAll, setIn, remove, getOrCompute, toMutable, toImmutable, toDataSet, clear all match. Companion merge() matches Java static merge(). getOrCompute correctly stores computed value (mutable semantics). No issues.

Java: 7 methods + getParent(). Scala: 6 methods + parent val. All override methods match (getAll, getKeys, toMutable, contains, getOrCompute). toMutable: Scala copies dataSet into new MutableDataSet then wraps in MutableScopedDataSet; Java copies super.getAll() - functionally equivalent since super.getAll returns dataSet. Minor difference: Scala toMutable passes Nullable(mutableDataSet.asInstanceOf[MutableDataHolder]) whereas Java passes mutableDataSet directly.

Java: 5 methods + getParent(). Scala: 4 methods + parent val. All override methods match (getAll, getKeys, contains, getOrCompute). Parent field as val replaces getter. Constructor takes Nullable params matching Java @Nullable. getAll and getKeys iterate parent keys correctly using while loops replacing for-each. No issues.

All 28 DataKey constants present and match Java original. EXTENSIONS uses Collections.emptyList (Java uses Extension.EMPTY_LIST - equivalent). ESCAPE_HEADING_NO_ATX_SPACE, ESCAPE_SPECIAL_CHARS, ESCAPE_NUMBERED_LEAD_IN, UNESCAPE_SPECIAL_CHARS all use DataNotNullValueFactory delegating to parent key matching Java method references. Types correctly mapped: Integer->Int, Boolean->Boolean, String->String. All default values match. No missing keys.
All 6 methods match. Trait faithfully ports Java interface.

1975 vs 4939 LoC (40%). Drives the full Compile pipeline: dispatches statements/expressions, @use/@forward with config, @media/@supports/@at-root bubbling, @each destructuring, @debug/@warn/@error, @extend with media scoping and !optional, first-class calc/min/max/clamp, custom props, parent & expansion, built-in dispatch, source maps. Honest gaps: textual (not AST) extend rewrite paths in several places, meta.apply stub, content-exists placeholder, synthetic error spans in sub-parsers, no mixin-call-stack tracking, many evaluator branches simplified vs dart. Drives typical SCSS correctly but not spec-compliant for extend edge cases, certain nested @use with-clauses, and advanced calc folding.

406 vs 1883 LoC (22%). Most severe LoC gap of the core visitors. Expanded + compressed styles, all 9 visit methods, !important, short hex/named-color collapse, SassNumber trailing-zero stripping, rgba legacy, SassCalculation rendering, minimal v3 source maps (per-rule/decl, base64 VLQ). Honest gaps: no sourcesContent/sourceRoot/file, per-node (not per-token) mapping granularity, no @import propagation of maps, missing many dart-sass edge cases around whitespace/comment preservation, compressed-mode micro-optimizations, unicode-range, supports modern syntax edge cases, CSS nesting selector rendering nuances.
Tokenizer fully implemented: whitespace/comments/identifier/string/declarationValue/escape/naturalNumber. 6/6 tests passing.

MVP text-based expression parser, 2367 vs 4887 LoC (48%). Covers all common SCSS at-rules (@mixin/@function/@include with rest+kwargs, @if/@for/@each/@while, @media/@supports, @at-root, @keyframes, @debug/@warn/@error, @use/@forward with show/hide/as/with, @import, @extend !optional, CSS custom props, #{} interp, !important). Known gaps per SHORTCUTS: selectors stored as raw interpolation, no proper space-list/function-call tokenizer, rare edge cases can mis-split; error spans for sub-parsers are synthetic placeholders.
All abstract hooks implemented. Sequences statements, handles blocks, scanElse, statement separators.

162 vs 491 LoC (33%) plus 7 UnsupportedOperationException stubs. Indented syntax handled by a preprocessor translating to SCSS, so most statement-loop override hooks (styleRuleSelector, expectStatementSeparator, atEndOfStatement, lookingAtChildren, scanElse, children, statements) throw. Multi-line selector continuations not supported. Works for the common indented files but any file that relies on the statement-loop contract will fail.
